concerns.text: The validation target (modality label: 'text-only' vs 'image-only') is DEFINITIONALLY determined by the input region selection criteria (FR-003). The spec requires training a classifier to predict the label that was used to select the data. This is a circular validation: the model is not learning to distinguish latent features; it is learning to recognize the selection mask. A valid test requires the labels to be INDEPENDENT of the region selection (e.g., predicting semantic content from a blind crop) or the hypothesis must be reframed as 'can the VAE encode the selection mask information'.

The validation metric (SSIM ≥ 0.85) is compared against a 'ground-truth layout reference' or 'bounding box geometry'. However, the editing operation ($z_{new} = z_{doc} - \mu_{text old} + \mu_{text new}$) explicitly preserves the non-text components of $z_{doc}$. If the 'layout' is defined by the non-text latent components, the high SSIM is a mathematical identity of the subtraction operation, not an empirical finding of the VAE's disentanglement. The validation target is trivially determined by the predictor's construction.

Applying Bonferroni correction to 'accuracy, F1, SSIM' is methodologically unsound. These are distinct metrics measuring different properties (classification performance vs. reconstruction fidelity). Bonferroni is for correcting p-values across multiple *hypothesis tests* of the same underlying null hypothesis. Correcting across different metric types conflates distinct statistical questions and reduces power without justification. The spec must define specific null hypotheses for each test before applying corrections.

The assumption that 'text mean' and 'image mean' vectors are stable and representative assumes the very disentanglement the project seeks to prove. If the latent space is entangled, these centroids will be noisy and the vector arithmetic will fail. The methodology relies on a prior existence proof of the geometric structure it is trying to discover, creating a circular dependency in the experimental design.
